mechanism_narrative: A significant year-over-year contraction in housing starts indicates a severe downturn in the construction and real estate sectors, with negative wealth effects and spillovers to the broader economy, depressing corporate earnings expectations and leading to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Subprime mortgage crisis unfolding since mid-2007
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, which reduces consumer income and confidence. This leads to lower aggregate demand and corporate profits, causing negative forward equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE has risen from 4.4% in March 2007 to 4.7% in October 2007
failure_condition: The 3-month change in UNRATE has a rolling 36-month rank correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns that is not statistically significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve cuts the Fed Funds rate while the unemployment rate is simultaneously rising, it signals that monetary policy is reacting to, rather than pre-empting, a significant economic downturn. This confirmation of weakness leads to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed cut rates in Sep/Oct 2007 while UNRATE was rising
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger is active, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating year-over-year growth in industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing and business activity. This directly implies lower future corporate revenue and earnings growth, leading to negative pressure on equity valuations over the medium term.
failure_condition: The 12-month growth rate of INDPRO fails to show a statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation exceeds a critical threshold, it erodes the real value of future corporate earnings and may constrain the central bank's ability to ease policy, increasing the equity risk premium and causing negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI y/y was 3.5% in Oct 2007, up from 2.0% in Aug
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the average 20-day forward S&P 500 return is not statistically lower than the average return when the condition is not met.

mechanism_narrative: The month-over-month growth rate of retail sales is a direct measure of consumer spending momentum. A negative growth rate signals imminent weakness in corporate revenues for consumer-facing sectors, leading to negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 1-month growth in RSAFS and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is not positive and significant over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the 3-month average change in nonfarm payrolls falls below a key level required to absorb new labor force entrants, it signals the onset of a recessionary labor market dynamic, causing a sharp repricing of equity risk.
candidate_trigger: 3-month moving average of PAYEMS monthly change < 75000
evidence_refs: fact:Payroll growth has been weakening throughout 2007
failure_condition: In the 6 months following a trigger event, the cumulative S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: An inverted or rapidly flattening yield curve, proxied by a narrowing spread between long-term growth expectations (GDPC1) and the short-term policy rate (FEDFUNDS), signals monetary policy is restrictive relative to economic fundamentals, leading to negative returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rate > annualized q/q GDPC1 growth rate
evidence_refs: fact:This condition was met for much of 2006-2007, preceding market weakness
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward S&P 500 return under the trigger condition is not statistically significantly lower than the average return when the trigger is not active.
